item.title: Iran, U.S. ramp up tit-for-tat strikes ahead of slain leader's burial
item.description: Iran's former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei is being buried amid an escalating exchange of fire on the 132nd day of the war.

item.title: Venezuelans reunite with pets rescued by volunteers after deadly earthquakes
item.description: Cleanup and recovery efforts are underway after two deadly earthquakes devastated Venezuela, destroying homes and killing thousands. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez spoke with animal rescue volunteers working to save pets and ignite hope.

item.title: Oil prices seesaw after renewed tensions between U.S. and Iran
item.description: Oil prices are reacting to the re-escalation of the conflict between the U.S. and Iran. CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger has more.

item.title: Trump claims Iran "wants to make a deal so badly" after more strikes
item.description: President Trump told reporters early Thursday that Tehran "wants to make a deal so badly" after the U.S. and Iran traded more strikes. CBS News Olivia Rinaldi and Courtney Kealy have more.

item.title: Democrats scramble to find replacement after Graham Platner drops out of Maine Senate race
item.description: Democrat Graham Platner suspended his Senate campaign in Maine following a sexual assault allegation, which he vehemently denied. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns has more on the news, and political consultant Kristian Ramos also joins to discuss.

item.title: Army to share findings of Kuwait attack probe with Gold Star families
item.description: Six Americans were killed in the March 1 Iranian strike on a tactical operations center in Kuwait.

item.title: Who's responsible for credit card debt after a divorce?
item.description: Ending a marriage doesn't always end the financial responsibility you have for shared credit card obligations.

item.title: Inside the "Big Brother" season 28 house with host Julie Chen Moonves
item.description: Julie Chen Moonves, who hosts "Big Brother," gives "CBS Mornings" a sneak peek inside the house ahead of the season 28 premiere.

item.title: White House announces "Freedom Fuel" gas station selling $3.47 gasoline
item.description: The Freedom Fuel Network is offering gasoline for more than 30 cents a gallon below the national average.

item.title: Eva Marcille on the fifth and final season of "All the Queen's Men"
item.description: Eva Marcille, who stars in "All the Queen's Men," joins "CBS Mornings" to discuss the show's fifth and final season.

item.title: Bodycam video shows day when Arizona toddler was declared dead, then found alive in hospital morgue
item.description: A Phoenix toddler found in a backyard pool was mistakenly declared dead by a doctor, despite two police officers and at least one nurse seeing possible signs of life. Hours later, the toddler was discovered alive in the hospital morgue. CBS News' Jericka Duncan has more.

item.title: Here's how modern families divide and conquer household chores, according to Good Housekeeping
item.description: Elspeth Velten, the editor-in-chief of Good Housekeeping, joins "CBS Mornings" to discuss a recent survey by the magazine showing how people feel about splitting household chores.

item.title: Clips of interview with roommate of Charlie Kirk murder suspect to be played in court
item.description: Prosecutors plan to play audio clips of an interview with the roommate of Charlie Kirk's accused killer during a preliminary hearing on Thursday. CBS News legal contributor Caroline Polisi has more details.

item.title: Olympian David Hearn pleads not guilty to charges of damaging Reflecting Pool
item.description: Three-time Olympian David Hearn was indicted July 2, after he was accused of ripping out a portion of the sealant from the bottom of the Reflecting Pool on June 19.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: How to identify transient global amnesia after Katie Couric reveals health scare
item.description: Katie Couric revealed Wednesday she was hospitalized for recent temporary memory loss, called transient global amnesia. Dr. Leah Croll, a neurologist at Maimonides Health, joins "CBS Mornings" with more on the condition.

item.title: Judge orders Indian billionaire to answer questions on whether was quid pro quo
item.description: The Justice Department in May announced it had dismissed criminal foreign bribery and securities fraud charges against Gautam Adani, India's richest man.

item.title: What's next in U.S-Iran war; slain supreme leader Khamenei to be buried
item.description: Former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is set to be buried after U.S. and Iran exchanged more strikes. CBS News' Aaron MacLean has more on the war and the Strait of Hormuz, and Courtney Kealy explains the political and spiritual messaging behind Khamenei's funeral.

item.title: Trump says Iran "wants to make a deal so badly" amid new strikes
item.description: The U.S. and Iran traded a barrage of strikes after negotiations fell apart. President Trump said Iran called him and wants "to make a deal so badly." CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi has more.

item.title: Temperatures forecast to reach up to 117 degrees in western heat wave
item.description: A blistering heat wave persisted in the western United States on Thursday, and temperatures are forecast to reach up to 117 degrees in some desert areas.

item.title: U.S. and Iran trade new strikes as oil prices rise in response to renewed fighting
item.description: The U.S. and Iran traded new attacks on Thursday ahead of the burial for Iran's former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. President Trump said Iran "wants to make a deal so badly" and threatened "much worse" strikes if Iran attacks ships again. CBS News' Ramy Inocencio has more details.

item.title: How Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee to replace Graham Platner
item.description: Maine Democrats are facing a ticking clock to find a new candidate to go up against GOP Sen. Susan Collins this fall, after Graham Platner dropped out of the race following a sexual assault allegation.

item.title: Millions of Americans drop Affordable Care Act coverage after premiums spike
item.description: Millions of Americans are paying more or have dropped their insurance entirely after Affordable Care Act subsidies expired and insurance costs surged. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder talks about the impact of high healthcare and insurance costs.

item.title: Startup company reveals prototype of tsunami survival pod
item.description: LifePod says its survival device will be able to save small groups of children and adults from tsunamis and severe flooding.

item.title: What are today's CD interest rates?
item.description: When you open a CD, you'll lock in today's rates for the entire term. Here's how much you can earn.

item.title: What are today's HELOC and home equity loan interest rates?
item.description: HELOCs and home equity loans offer homeowners an affordable way to borrow money now. Here are the rates for each.

item.title: Kia recalling almost 463,000 Tellurides that may catch fire in motion or parked
item.description: Kia America is recalling almost 463,000 Telluride SUVs from the 2020-2024 model years that could catch fire while being driven or parked, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration says.

item.title: Bonnie Tyler, who sang "Total Eclipse of the Heart," dies at 75
item.description: Bonnie Tyler, the Welsh pop star best known for singing the chart-topping power ballad "Total Eclipse of the Heart" in 1983, has died. She was 75.

item.title: Cartel violence hits World Cup despite warnings: "Getting massacred"
item.description: The attacks came as Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has spent months grappling with how to address endemic criminal violence in Mexico.

item.title: Some large ICE facilities haven't been inspected in over a year
item.description: Detention facilities are now inspected every year or every other year rather than twice a year, a CBS News analysis of inspection reports found.
